revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing misc last stuff

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] }}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] }}

{{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] }}

{{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
Removing from [[Category:Second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Text replacement - "{{draft categories|" to "<noinclude> {{Draft categories| [[Category:templates with draft categories]]"
Text replacement - "[[Category:templates with draft categories fixed]]" to "<noinclude>[[Category:templates with draft categories fixed 3]]</noinclude>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:templates with draft categories]] [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }}\n

{{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{draft categories| [[Category:templates with draft categories]] [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }}\n

{{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:templates with draft categories]] [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n

revision.comment: Bot: redirect unused template to en:
revision.text: #redirect [[en:Template:{subst:PAGENAME}]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[en:Template:{subst:PAGENAME}]]" to "[[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]]"
revision.text: #redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]]" to "<noinclude>[[Category:Templates to undo 3 revisions on]]</noinclude>"
revision.text: #redirect <noinclude>[[Category:Templates to undo 3 revisions on]]</noinclude> [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect <noinclude>[[Category:Templates to undo 3 revisions on]]</noinclude> [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[en:Template:{subst:PAGENAME}]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect <noinclude>[[Category:Templates to undo 3 revisions on]]</noinclude> [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]

#redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[en:Template:{subst:PAGENAME}]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]

#redirect [[Category:templates to undo 2 revisions on]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]
#redirect [[en:Template:{subst:PAGENAME}]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia template redirects D]]

{{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:templates with draft categories]] [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:templates with draft categories]] [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Templates with draft categories
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

{{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23388260132|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23931424921|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Deprecated_code%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24926181992|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage|[[Template:Deprecated code]] ([[Template:Dc]]) and [[Template:Dc2]]}} {{Template shortcut|dc}} == Usage == The {{tlx|deprecated code}} template (easiest used from its '''{{tlx|dc}}''' redirect) can be used to indicate, e.g. in template documentation or Wikipedia articles on things like HTML specifications, code that has been [[:en:Deprecation|deprecated]] and should not normally be used. It can also be used to indicate other deleted or deprecated material. On the technical level it is a CSS-styled {{tag|del}} that greys out the text (the near-universal sign in computing and computing documentation for "doesn't work", "don't do this", "bad code", "ignore", "option not available", etc.), and removes that element's usual strikethrough (CSS: "line-through") rendering, which makes the content hard to read. If you really want that line-through, use the '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' (AKA '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') variant {{dc2|to do this}}; it is otherwise identical. {{strong|Note: This template does not apply a monospaced font.}} This is so that it can be used inside an existing code (or non-code) block and inherit its font style. If necessary, wrap the template in one of {{tag|code}} (source code), {{tag|kbd}} (example input), or {{tag|samp}} (example output) as [[:en:Semantic HTML|semantically]] appropriate. If you want monospace without semantic markup like {{tag|code|o}}, you can use the alternative templates {{tlx|mxtd}}: {{mxtd}}, or {{tlx|!mxt}}: {{!mxt}}. ===Parameters=== *{{para|1}}   the content to be marked as deprecated *{{para|2}} or {{para|title}} a mouse-over "tooltip" (in some browsers, anyway), e.g. for briefly explaining the deprecation, e.g. "Deprecated since HTML 3.0" or "Breaks infobox formatting" *{{para|red|y}}   make the text red instead of grey, for indicating dangerous/error/forbidden things (a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dcr}}''') *{{para|class}}   assign a CSS class *{{para|id}}   assign an HTML ID for #linking and other purposes (must be unique on the page and start with an alphabetic letter) *{{para|style}}   add additional CSS styling (can be used to add {{code|lang=css|code=text-decoration:line-through;}} back in if you want that formatting; a shortcut for this is '''{{tlx|dc2}}''' or '''{{tlx|dcs}}''') ===Examples=== *{{dc|deprecated}} yields: {{dc|deprecated}} *{{dcr|deprecated}} yields: {{dcr|deprecated}} *{{dc2|deprecated}} yields: {{dc2|deprecated}} *{{dcs|deprecated}} yields: {{dcs|deprecated}} == See also == * {{tlx|xtd}} and [[Template:Xt/see also|related templates]] for indicating deprecated, good, bad, and neutral examples in guidelines, how-tos, and template documentation. ** {{tlx|mxtd}} in this template group ({{mxtd|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dc}}. ** {{tlx|!mxt}} in this template group ({{!mxt|example output here}}) is an alternative to {{tlx|dcr}}. * {{tlx|strongbad}} – for introducing something as deprecated or issuing some other warning in documentation, e.g.: {{tlx|strongbad|Not for use in mainspace.}} {{Semantic markup templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} \n{{Draft categories| [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Semantic markup templates]] [[Category:Text color templates]] }} \n [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
